name: Wheatberry Pizza Dough
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine wheatberries and 1 cup water in a small bowl. Cover. Refrigerate overnight. Drain and set aside.
2. Combine yeast and 1/4 cup water in a small bowl and let stand until dissolved.
3. Combine salt and 2 cups lukewarm water in another bowl.
4. In yet another bowl, combine the whole wheat flour and 2 cups white flour.
5. Make a well in the dry ingredients bowl and add yeast mixture and salt mixture. Add the remaining white flour gradually to make a soft dough.
6. Knead on a lightly floured surface for 8-10 minutes, adding more flour as necessary to prevent sticking.
7. Place dough in a Pam-sprayed bowl and turn dough to coat with spray.
8. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise until doubled (1-2 hours).
9. Punch down dough. At this point you can freeze portions for up to 2 months. Bring dough to room temperature before using.
Makes 8 6-inch crusts.
302 calories/crust, 9 grams protein, 1 gram fat, 64 grams carbohydrates, 268 milligrams sodium, 0 milligrams cholesterol.
To make pizza crusts:
1. Heat oven to the hottest baking setting. Place rack in the lowest position. Place an inverted cooking sheet or tiles or pizza stone on the rack to heat.
2. Divide dough into 8 sections (or less if using a smaller batch).
3. Roll dough section into a round using a rolling pin. I found using a rolling pin makes a round shape.
4. Place on an inverted cornmeal-dusted baking sheet. Add your toppings.
5. Slide the unbaked pizza from the baking sheet into the oven with the heated baking sheet.
6. Bake for 10-14 minutes or until it looks done. This depends on your oven temperature, so keep a good eye on it the first few times you do this.
